findings.evidence: `rg` shows `theorem integral_blockIndicatorProd {μ : Measure Ω} ...` at line 146 with RHS `(blockLaw μ X k (Set.univ.pi C)).toReal`; its proof uses `setIntegral_const`/`Measure.real`, and Mathlib defines `Measure.real μ s := (μ s).toReal`, so infinite mass is collapsed to `0`.
findings.fix: Add `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` to `integral_blockIndicatorProd` (matching `integrable_blockIndicatorProd` and the downstream probability setting), or otherwise require the relevant block measure/integrand is finite/integrable; keep the arbitrary-measure statement only in the `lintegral` theorem.
findings.issue: `integral_blockIndicatorProd` has no `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` or finiteness/integrability hypothesis, so for an infinite block cylinder the public theorem rewrites the Bochner integral to `(∞).toReal = 0`, exposing Lean's nonintegrable-integral convention as if it were the intended probability identity.

summary: The real-integral theorem exposes an arbitrary-measure API even though its intended and mathematically usable form is finite-measure/integrable; this makes nonintegrable infinite cases silently rewrite to `ENNReal.toReal ∞ = 0`. Tighten the public statement to the finite-measure scope used by the target.
